content.full_text:
Stofe of Colifofl1ia 
Memorandum 
Date 
To 
, Advice Request Meeting Participants 
May 30, 1984 
No. T-84-l48 
ANSWER 
Jeanne P. said that we have always 
broadly interpreted "send." 
From 
I 
FAIR POUTICAl PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Senate Rules is prohibited from 
providing reprographics and 
mailing list. 
Subject I 
• 
Janis Shank McLean 
Mass Mailings 
FACTS: 
A member of the Senate, who is a candidateJprovided the Senate 
Rules Committee with a brochure and asked it to reproduce the 
brochure and to provide him with the Senate mailing list. A third 
party will pay the cost of mailing the brochure. 
STATUTORY PROVISIONS: 
Section 89001 prohibits a legislative newsletter or other mass 
mailing from being sent at public e~pense by or on behalf of any 
elected state officer after the elected state officer has filed a 
declaration of candidacy for any office. 
" 
QUESTIONS PRESENTED: 
Can a third party pay for this mailing? 
If so, can the Senate Rules Committee provide the reproduction 
and mailing list? 
DISCUSSION: 
It seems to me that the third party can pay for the mailing, as 
long as the expenditure for the postage is properly reported. 
However, I do not feel that the Senate Rules Committee should be 
allowed to reproduce the brochure or provide the mailing list. 
One 
way to prohibit this activity would be to interpret the prohibition 
in Section 89001 (against sending a mass mailing at public expense) 
to include every aspect of sending the mailing (reproduction, 
acquisition of mailing lists, postage, etc.)~ rather than just the 
cost of postage. 
(I have checked the file and did not find any 
letters on this issue.) 
Lynn has suggested that, in providing the reproduction and 
mailing lists, the Committee is making a contribution which may be 
prohibited by rules outside of the PRA. 
What do you think? 
JSM:plh

If so, can the Senate Rules Committee provide the reproduction 
and mailing list?
sections.conclusion: Senate Rules is prohibited from providing reprographics and mailing list.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a third party can fund a mass mailing by a Senate member candidate, and whether the Senate Rules Committee can provide reproduction services and mailing lists for such a mailing.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Senate Rules Committee is prohibited from providing reproduction services and mailing lists. While a third party may pay for postage if properly reported, the Committee's provision of reproduction and mailing list services should be prohibited as they constitute public expense contributions under Section 89001.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document appears to be internal FPPC memorandum from meeting participants. Contains clear QUESTIONS PRESENTED and ANSWER sections. Some OCR quality issues (e.g., 'Stofe' for 'State', 'Colifofl1ia' for 'California'). Discussion section shows deliberative reasoning. Conclusion is somewhat implicit in the ANSWER section and discussion rather than formally labeled as CONCLUSION.

If so, can the Senate Rules Committee provide the reproduction 
and mailing list?

CONCLUSION: Senate Rules is prohibited from providing reprographics and mailing list.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice on whether a Senate member candidate can use Senate Rules Committee resources (reproduction and mailing list) for a mass mailing funded by a third party. The Commission concludes that while third-party funding of postage is permissible if reported, the Committee cannot provide reproduction or mailing list services.
